questions.questionText: In an ac circuit, an inductor, a capacitor and a resistor are connected in series with X<sub>L</sub> = R = X<sub>C</sub>. Impedance of this circuit is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$Z = \sqrt {{{({X_L} - {X_C})}^2} + {R^2}} = R$$ $$\because$$ XL = XC

Option (c)

questions.questionText:
A sample of a radioactive nucleus A disintegrates to another radioactive nucleus B, which in turn disintegrates to some other stable nucleus C. Plot of a graph showing the variation of number of atoms of nucleus B versus time is :

(Assume that at t = 0, there are no B atoms in the sample)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
A $$\to$$ B $$\to$$ C (stable)

Initially no. of atoms of B = 0 at t = 0, no. of atoms of B will starts increasing & reaches maximum value when rate of decay of B = rate of formation of B.

After that maximum value, no. of atoms will starts decreasing as growth & decay both are exponential functions, so best possible graph is (d).

questions.questionText: A body of mass M moving at speed V<sub>0</sub> collides elastically with a mass 'm' at rest. After the collision, the two masses move at angles $$\theta$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\theta$$<sub>2</sub> with respect to the initial direction of motion of the body of mass M. The largest possible value of the ratio M/m, for which the angles $$\theta$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\theta$$<sub>2</sub> will be equal, is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given $$\theta$$1 = $$\theta$$2 = $$\theta$$

from momentum conservation

in x-direction MV0 = MV1 cos$$\theta$$ + mV2 cos$$\theta$$

in y-direction 0 = MV1 sin$$\theta$$ $$-$$ mV2 sin$$\theta$$

Solving above equations

$${V_2} = {{M{V_1}} \over m}$$, V0 = 2V1 cos$$\theta$$

From energy conservation

$${1 \over 2}MV_0^2 = {1 \over 2}MV_1^2 + {1 \over 2}MV_2^2$$

Substituting value of V2 & V0, we will get

$${M \over m} + 1 = 4{\cos ^2}\theta \le 4$$

$${M \over m} \le 3$$

Option (c)

questions.questionText: Consider a galvanometer shunted with 5$$\Omega$$ resistance and 2% of current passes through it. What is the resistance of the given galvanometer ?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
0.02i Rg = 0.98i $$\times$$ 5

Rg = 245 $$\Omega$$

Option (c)

questions.questionText: A moving proton and electron have the same de-Broglie wavelength. If K and P denote the K.E. and momentum respectively. Then choose the correct option :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${\lambda _p} = {h \over {{P_p}}}$$

$${\lambda _e} = {h \over {{P_e}}}$$

$$\because$$ $${\lambda _p} = {\lambda _e}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {P_p} = {P_e}$$

$${(K)_p} = {{P_p^2} \over {2{m_p}}}$$

$${(K)_e} = {{P_e^2} \over {2{m_e}}}$$

Kp < Ke as mp > me

Option (a)

questions.questionText: A small square loop of side 'a' and one turn is placed inside a larger square loop of side b and one turn (b >> a). The two loops are coplanar with their centres coinciding. If a current I is passed in the square loop of side 'b', then the coefficient of mutual inductance between the two loops is :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$B = \left[ {{{{\mu _0}} \over {4\pi }}{I \over {b/2}} \times 2\sin 45} \right] \times 4$$

$$\phi = 2\sqrt 2 {{{\mu _0}} \over \pi }{I \over b} \times {a^2}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$M = {\phi \over I} = {{2\sqrt 2 {\mu _0}{a^2}} \over {\pi b}} = {{{\mu _0}} \over {4\pi }}8\sqrt 2 {{{a^2}} \over b}$$

Option (a)

questions.questionText: In the following logic circuit the sequence of the inputs A, B are (0, 0), (0, 1), (1, 0) and (1, 1). The output Y for this sequence will be :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$Y = \overline {(A\,.\,B)\,.\,(A + B)} $$

$${\left. Y \right)_{(0,0)}} = 1$$

$${\left. Y \right)_{(0,1)}} = 1$$

$${\left. Y \right)_{(1,0)}} = 1$$

$${\left. Y \right)_{(1,1)}} = 0$$

Option (c) is correct.

questions.questionText: Choose the correct waveform that can represent the voltage across R of the following circuit, assuming the diode is ideal one :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
When Vi > 3 volt, VR > 0

Because diode will be in forward biased state

When Vi $$\le$$ 3 volt ; VR = 0

Because diode will be in reverse biased state.

questions.questionText: Two particles A and B having charges 20$$\mu$$C and $$-$$5$$\mu$$C respectively are held fixed with a separation of 5 cm. At what position a third charged particle should be placed so that it does not experience a net electric force?
options: At 5 cm from 20 $$\mu$$C on the left side of system
At 5 cm from $$-$$5 $$\mu$$C on the right side
At 1.25 cm from $$-$$5 $$\mu$$C between two charges
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Null point is possible only right side of $$-$$5 $$\mu$$C



$${E_N} = + {{k( - 5\mu C)} \over {{x^2}}} + {{k(20\mu C)} \over {{{(5 + x)}^2}}} = 0$$

x = 5 cm

$$\therefore$$ Option (b) is correct.

questions.questionText: An object is placed at the focus of concave lens having focal length f. What is the magnification and distance of the image from the optical centre of the lens?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
U = $$-$$f

$${1 \over V} - {1 \over U} = {1 \over { - f}} \Rightarrow {1 \over V} = - {2 \over f}$$

$$V = {{ - f} \over 2}$$

$$m = {V \over U} = {1 \over 2}$$

distance = $${f \over 2}$$

Option (c)

questions.questionText: Two plane mirrors M1 and M2 are at right angle to each other shown. A point source 'P' is placed at 'a' and '2a' meter away from M1 and M2 respectively. The shortest distance between the images thus formed is : (Take $$\sqrt 5 $$ = 2.3)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Shortest distance is 2a between I1 & I3

But answer given is for I1 & I2

= $$\sqrt {{{(4a)}^2} + {{(2a)}^2}} $$

= $$a\sqrt {20} $$

= 4.47 a

Option (b)

questions.questionText: The masses and radii of the earth and moon are (M<sub>1</sub>, R<sub>1</sub>) and (M<sub>2</sub>, R<sub>2</sub>) respectively. Their centres are at a distance 'r' apart. Find the minimum escape velocity for a particle of mass 'm' to be projected from the middle of these two masses :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${1 \over 2}m{V^2} - {{G{M_1}m} \over {r/2}} - {{G{M_2}m} \over {r/2}} = 0$$

$${1 \over 2}m{V^2} = {{2Gm} \over r}({M_1} + {M_2})$$

$$V = \sqrt {{{4G({M_1} + {M_2})} \over r}} $$

Option (b)

questions.questionText: A reversible engine has an efficiency of $${1 \over 4}$$. If the temperature of the sink is reduced by 58$$^\circ$$C, its efficiency becomes double. Calculate the temperature of the sink :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: For an ideal gas the instantaneous change in pressure 'p' with volume 'v' is given by the equation $${{dp} \over {dv}} =  - ap$$. If p = p<sub>0</sub> at  v =0 is the given boundary condition, then the maximum temperature one mole of gas can attain is : (Here R is the gas constant)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$\int\limits_{{p_0}}^p {{{dp} \over P} = - a\int\limits_0^v {dv} } $$

$$\ln \left( {{p \over {{p_0}}}} \right) = - av$$

$$p = {p_0}{e^{ - av}}$$

For temperature maximum p-v product should be maximum

$$T = {{pv} \over {nR}} = {{{p_0}v{e^{ - av}}} \over R}$$

$${{dT} \over {dv}} = 0 \Rightarrow {{{p_0}} \over R}\{ {e^{ - av}} + v{e^{ - av}}( - a)\} $$ = 0

$${{{p_0}{e^{ - av}}} \over R}\{ 1 - av\} = 0$$

$$v = {1 \over a},\infty $$

$$T = {{{p_0}1} \over {Rae}} = {{{p_0}} \over {Rae}}$$

at v = $$\infty$$

T = 0

Option (a)

questions.questionText: A coil having N turns is wound tightly in the form of a spiral with inner and outer radii 'a' and 'b' respectively. Find the magnetic field at centre, when a current I passes through coil:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
No. of turns in dx width = $${N \over {b - a}}dx$$

$$\int {dB = \int\limits_a^b {\left( {{N \over {b - a}}} \right)dx{{{\mu _0}I} \over {2x}}} } $$

$$B = {{N{\mu _0}i} \over {2(b - a)}}\ln \left( {{b \over a}} \right)$$

Option (a)

questions.questionText: A helicopter is flying horizontally with a speed 'v' at an altitude 'h' has to drop a food packet for a man on the ground. What is the distance of helicopter from the man when the food packet is dropped?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$R = \sqrt {{{2h} \over g}} .\,v$$

$$D = \sqrt {{R^2} + {h^2}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {{{\left( {\sqrt {{{2h} \over g}} .\,v} \right)}^2} + {h^2}} $$

$$D = \sqrt {{{2h{v^2}} \over g} + {h^2}} $$

Option (c) is correct.

questions.questionText: A uniform heavy rod of weight 10 kg ms<sup>$$-$$2</sup>, cross-sectional area 100 cm<sup>2</sup> and length 20 cm is hanging from a fixed support. Young modulus of the material of the rod is 2 $$\times$$ 10<sup>11</sup> Nm<sup>$$-$$2</sup>. Neglecting the lateral contraction, find the elongation of rod due to its own weight.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
We know,

$$\Delta l = {{WL} \over {2AY}}$$

$$\Delta l = {{10 \times 1} \over {2 \times 5}} \times 100 \times {10^{ - 4}} \times 2 \times {10^{11}}$$

$$\Delta l = {1 \over 2} \times {10^{ - 9}} = 5 \times {10^{ - 10}}$$ m

Option (d)

questions.questionText: Angular momentum of a single particle moving with constant speed along circular path :
options: changes in magnitude but remains same in the direction
remains same in magnitude and direction
remains same in magnitude but changes in the direction
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$\left| {\overrightarrow L } \right|$$ = mvr

And direction will be upward & remain constant.

Option (b)

questions.questionText:
Match List - I with List - II.

List - I List - II
(a) Torque (i) MLT$$^{ - 1}$$
(b) Impulse (ii) MT$$^{ - 2}$$
(c) Tension (iii) ML$$^{ 2}$$T$$^{ - 2}$$
(d) Surface Tension (iv) MLT$$^{ - 2}$$


Choose the most appropriate answer from the option given below :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
torque $$\tau$$ $$\to$$ ML2T$$-$$2 (iii)

Impulse I $$\Rightarrow$$ MLT$$-$$1 (i)

Tension force $$\Rightarrow$$ MLT$$-$$2 (iv)

Surface tension $$\Rightarrow$$ MT$$-$$2 (ii)

Option (a)

questions.questionText:
Which of the following equations is dimensionally incorrect?

Where t = time, h = height, s = surface tension, $$\theta$$ = angle, $$\rho$$ = density, a, r = radius, g = acceleration due to gravity, v = volume, p = pressure, W = work done, T = torque, $$\in$$ = permittivity, E = electric field, J = current density, L = length.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
(a) $${{\pi p{a^4}} \over {8\eta L}} = {{dv} \over {dt}}$$ = Volumetric flow rate (Poiseuille's law)

(b) $$h\rho g = {{2s} \over r}\cos \theta $$

(c) $$\varepsilon \times {1 \over {4\pi {\varepsilon _0}}}{a \over {{r^2}}} \times {1 \over \varepsilon } = {q \over t} \times {1 \over {{r^2}}}$$

$$ = {1 \over {{L^2}}} = I{L^{ - 2}}$$

LHS

$$T = {I \over A} = I{L^{ - 2}}$$

(d) W = $$\tau$$$$\theta$$

Option (a)

questions.questionText: A capacitor of 50 $$\mu$$F is connected in a circuit as shown in figure. The charge on the upper plate of the capacitor is ______________$$\mu$$C.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Potential Difference across each resistor = 2V

q = CV

= 50 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 $$\times$$ 2 = 100 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$6 = 100 $$\mu$$C

questions.questionText: If the sum of the heights of transmitting and receiving antennas in the line of sight of communication is fixed at 160 m, then the maximum range of LOS communication is ___________ km. (Take radius of Earth = 6400 km)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
hT = hR = 160 ........ (i)

$$d = \sqrt {2R{h_T}} + \sqrt {2R{h_R}} $$

$$d = \sqrt {2R} \left[ {\sqrt {{h_T}} + \sqrt {{h_R}} } \right]$$

$$d = \sqrt {2R} \left[ {\sqrt x + \sqrt {160 - x} } \right]$$

$${{d(d)} \over {dx}} = 0$$

$${1 \over {2\sqrt x }} + {{1( - 1)} \over {2\sqrt {160 - x} }} = 0$$

$${1 \over {\sqrt x }} = {1 \over {\sqrt {160 - x} }}$$

x = 80 m

$${d_{\max }} = \sqrt {2 \times 6400} \left[ {\sqrt {{{80} \over {1000}}} + \sqrt {{{20} \over {1000}}} } \right]$$

$$ = {{80\sqrt 2 \times 2\sqrt {80} } \over {10\sqrt {10} }}$$

$$ = 8 \times 2 \times \sqrt 2 \times 2\sqrt 2 = 64$$ km

questions.questionText: A square shaped wire with resistance of each side 3$$\Omega$$ is bent to form a complete circle. The resistance between two diametrically opposite points of the circle in unit of $$\Omega$$ will be ___________.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution: Req = 3$$\Omega$$

questions.questionText: The voltage drop across 15$$\Omega$$ resistance in the given figure will be ______________ V.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$\Rightarrow$$ effective circuit diagram will be



Point drop across 6$$\Omega$$ = 1 $$\times$$ 6 = 6 = VAB

$$\Rightarrow$$ Hence point drop across 15$$\Omega$$ = 6 volt = VAB

questions.questionText:
The electric field in an electromagnetic wave is given by E = (50 NC$$-$$1) sin$$\omega$$ (t $$-$$ x/c)

The energy contained in a cylinder of volume V is 5.5 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$12 J. The value of V is _____________ cm3. (given $$\in$$0 = 8.8 $$\times$$ 10$$-$$12C2N$$-$$1m$$-$$2)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$E = 50\sin \left( {\omega t - {\omega \over c}.\,x} \right)$$

Energy density = $${1 \over 2}{ \in _0}E_0^2$$

Energy of volume $$V = {1 \over 2}{ \in _0}E_0^2.\,V = 5.5 \times {10^{ - 12}}$$

$${1 \over 2}8.8 \times {10^{ - 12}} \times 2500V = 5.5 \times {10^{ - 12}}$$

$$V = {{5.5 \times 2} \over {2500 \times 8.8}} = .0005{m^3}$$

= .0005 $$\times$$ 106 (c.m)3

= 500 (c.m)3

questions.questionText: A car is moving on a plane inclined at 30$$^\circ$$ to the horizontal with an acceleration of 10 ms<sup>$$-$$2</sup> parallel to the plane upward. A bob is suspended by a string from the roof of the car. The angle in degrees which the string makes with the vertical is ______________. (Take g = 10 ms<sup>$$-$$2</sup>)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given,

Angle of inclination, $$\theta$$ = 30$$^\circ$$

Acceleration, a = 10 ms$$-$$2

Acceleration due to gravity, g = 10 ms$$-$$2

According to the question the car and bob is as shown below,

Here, F' is the pseudo force acting on the bob when we considered it from car's frame and T is the tension on the string.

In equilibrium, $$\Sigma$$Fx = 0 and $$\Sigma$$Fy = 0

$$\Rightarrow$$ F' cos30$$^\circ$$ = T sin$$\alpha$$

$${{ma\cos 30^\circ } \over {\sin \alpha }} = T$$ ...... (i)

where, m is the mass of the bob.

$$F'\sin 30^\circ + mg = T\cos \alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow ma\sin 30^\circ + mg = {{ma\cos 30^\circ } \over {\sin \alpha }}(\cos \alpha )$$ [$$\because$$ using Eq. (i)]

$$ \Rightarrow a\sin 30^\circ + g = {{a\cos 30^\circ } \over {\sin \alpha }}(\cos \alpha )$$

$$10 \times {1 \over 2} + 10 = {{10 \times \sqrt 3 } \over 2}\cot \alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over 2} + 1 = {{\sqrt 3 } \over 2}\cot \alpha \Rightarrow {3 \over 2} = {{\sqrt 3 } \over 2}\cot \alpha $$

or, $$\cot \alpha = \sqrt 3 $$

$$ \Rightarrow \alpha = 30^\circ $$

questions.questionText:
When a rubber ball is taken to a depth of __________m in deep sea, its volume decreases by 0.5%.

(The bulk modulus of rubber = 9.8 $$\times$$ 108 Nm$$-$$2, Density of sea water = 103 kgm$$-$$3, g = 9.8 m/s2)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$$B = - {{\Delta P} \over {\left( {{{\Delta V} \over V}} \right)}} = - {{\rho gh} \over {\left( {{{\Delta V} \over V}} \right)}}$$

$$ - {{B{{\Delta V} \over V}} \over {\rho g}} = h$$

$${{9.8 \times {{10}^8} \times 0.5} \over {100 \times {{10}^3} \times 9.8}} = h$$

h = 500

questions.questionText: A particle of mass 1 kg is hanging from a spring of force constant 100 Nm<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. The mass is pulled slightly downward and released so that it executes free simple harmonic motion with time period T. The time when the kinetic energy and potential energy of the system will become equal, is $${T \over x}$$. The value of x is _____________.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
KE = PE

$$y = {A \over {\sqrt 2 }} = A\sin \omega t$$


$$t = {T \over 8} = {T \over x}$$

x = 8

questions.questionText: A wire having a linear mass density 9.0 $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$4</sup> kg/m is stretched between two rigid supports with a tension of 900 N. The wire resonates at a frequency of 500 Hz. The next higher frequency at which the same wire resonates is 550 Hz. The length of the wire is ____________ m.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A block moving horizontally on a smooth surface with a speed of 40 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup> splits into two equal parts. If one of the parts moves at 60 ms<sup>$$-$$1</sup> in the same direction, then the fractional change in the kinetic energy will be x : 4 where x = ___________.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Pi = Pf

m $$\times$$ 40 = $${m \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ v + $${m \over 2}$$ $$\times$$ 60

40 = $${v \over 2}$$ + 30

$$\Rightarrow$$ v = 20

(K. E.)I = $${1 \over 2}$$m $$\times$$ (40)2 = 800 m

(K. E.)f = $${1 \over 2}$$$${m \over 2}$$ . (20)2 + $${1 \over 2}$$ . $${m \over 2}$$ (60)2 = 1000 m

| $$\Delta$$ K. E. | = | 1000m $$-$$ 800 m | = 200 m

$${{\Delta K.E.} \over {{{(K.E.)}_i}}} = {{200m} \over {800m}} = {1 \over 4} = {x \over 4}$$

x = 1

questions.questionText: The major product formed in the following reaction is :

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Treatment of bromine water with propene yields 1-bromopropan-2-ol.

Reason (R) : Attack of water on bromonium ion follows Markovnikov rule and results in 1-bromopropan-2-ol.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Its IUPAC name 1-bromopropan-2-ol

A and R are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R) :

Assertion (A) : A simple distillation can be used to separate a mixture of propanol and propanone.

Reason (R) : Two liquids with a difference of more than 20$$^\circ$$ in their boiling points can be separated by simple distillations.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Simple distillation can be used to separate a mixture of propanol and propanone.

• Two liquid with difference in B.P. around 20°C can be separated by simple distillation.

• B.P. of propanol is 370 K.
B.P. of acetone is 329 K.

Both assertion & reason are correct & (R) is the correct explanation of (A).

questions.questionText: Which one of the following compounds contains $$\beta$$-C<sub>1</sub>-C<sub>4</sub> glycosidic linkage?

In Lactose it is $$\beta$$ C1 - C4 glycosidic linkage.

In Maltose, Amylose $$\alpha$$ C1 - C4 glycosidic linkage is present.

Acetate in acetic acid with R-X cause SN1

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The process of producing syn-gas is called gasification of coal.

Statement II : The composition of syn-gas is CO + CO2 + H2 (1 : 1 : 1)

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true

questions.solution:
The process of producing syn-gas from coal is called gasification of coal.

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Aluminium is extracted from bauxite by the electrolysis of molten mixture of Al2O3 with cryolite.

Reason (R) : The oxidation state of Al in cryolite is +3.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(A) Aluminium is reactive metal so Aluminium is extracted by electrolysis of Alumina with molten mixture of Cryolite

(B) Cryolite, Na3AlF6

Here, Al is in +3 O.S.

So, answer is (d).

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : Metallic character decreases and non-metallic character increases on moving from left to right in a period.

Reason (R) : It is due to increase in ionisation enthalpy and decrease in electron gain enthalpy, when one moves from left to right in a period.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
From left to right in periodic table :-

Metallic character decreases

Non-metallic character increases

$$\Rightarrow$$ It is due to increase in ionization enthalpy and increase in electron gain enthalpy.

questions.questionText: Which one of the following 0.10 M aqueous solutions will exhibit the largest freezing point depression?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
ΔTf $$ \propto $$ i × m

greater the value of i, greater will be the ΔTf value.

Van't Hoff factor is highest for KHSO4

$$\therefore$$ colligative property ($$\Delta$$Tf) will be highest for KHSO4

questions.questionText:
Select the graph that correctly describes the adsorption isotherms at two temperatures T1, and T2 (T1 > T2) for a gas :

(x - mass of the gas absorbed ; m - mass of adsorbent ; P - pressure)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
According to Freundlich adsorption isotherm, in the median range of pressure

       $${x \over m} \propto {P^{{1 \over n}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${x \over m}$$ = kP$$^{{1 \over n}}$$

On increasing temperature $${x \over m}$$ decreases.

$$\because$$ Adsorption is generally exothermic in nature. Hence, correct option is (A).

questions.questionText: The number of hydrogen bonded water molecule(s) associated with stoichiometry CuSO<sub>4</sub>.5H<sub>2</sub>O is ____________.

questions.solution: One hydrogen bonded H2O molecule

questions.questionText: For a first order reaction, the ratio of the time for 75% completion of a reaction to the time for 50% completion is ____________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
According to the following figure, the magnitude of the enthalpy change of the reaction

A + B $$\to$$ M + N in kJ mol$$-$$ is equal to ___________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$$\Delta$$H = EProduct $$-$$ EReactant

= 15 $$-$$ (15 + 45)

= $$-$$45 KJ/mol

| $$\Delta$$H | = 45 KJ/mol

questions.questionText:
The total number of reagents from those given blow, that can convert nitrobenzene into aniline is ___________. (Integer answer)

I. Sn $$-$$ HCl

II. Sn $$-$$ NH4OH

III. Fe $$-$$ HCl

IV. Zn $$-$$ HCl

V. H2 $$-$$ Pd

VI. H2 $$-$$ Raney Nickel
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
(i) Sn + HCl

(ii) Fe + HCl

(iii) Zn + HCl

(iv) H2 $$-$$ Pd

(v) H2 (Raney Ni)

questions.questionText:
Consider the following cell reaction :

$$C{d_{(s)}} + H{g_2}S{O_{4(s)}} + {9 \over 5}{H_2}{O_{(l)}}$$ $$\rightleftharpoons$$ $$CdS{O_4}.{9 \over 5}{H_2}{O_{(s)}} + 2H{g_{(l)}}$$

The value of $$E_{cell}^0$$ is 4.315 V at 25$$^\circ$$C. If $$\Delta$$H$$^\circ$$ = $$-$$825.2 kJ mol$$-$$1, the standard entropy change $$\Delta$$S$$^\circ$$ in J K$$-$$1 is ___________. (Nearest integer) [Given : Faraday constant = 96487 C mol$$-$$1]
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
$$\Delta G^\circ = - nFE^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ $$

$$ \therefore $$ $$\Delta$$S$$^\circ$$ $$ = {{\Delta H^\circ + nFE^\circ } \over T}$$

$$ = {{( - 825.2 \times {{10}^3}) + (2 \times 96487 \times 4.315)} \over {298}}$$

$$ = {{ - 825.2 \times {{10}^3} + 832.682 \times {{10}^3}} \over {298}}$$

$$ = {{7.483 \times {{10}^3}} \over {298}} = 25.11$$ JK$$-$$1 mol$$-$$1

$$\therefore$$ Nearest integer answer is 25.

questions.questionText: A<sub>3</sub>B<sub>2</sub> is a sparingly soluble salt of molar mass M (g mol<sup>$$-$$1</sup>) and solubility x g L<sup>$$-$$1</sup>. The solubility product satisfies $${K_{sp}} = a{\left( {{x \over M}} \right)^5}$$. The value of a is _____________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
KSP = (3s)3 (2s)2

KSP = 108 s5 & s = (x/M)

KSP = 108$${\left( {{x \over M}} \right)^5}$$

given $${K_{sp}} = a{\left( {{x \over M}} \right)^5}$$

comparing a = 108

questions.questionText: Consider the sulphides HgS, PbS, CuS, Sb<sub>2</sub>S<sub>3</sub>, As<sub>2</sub>S<sub>3</sub> and CdS. Number of these sulphides soluble in 50% HNO<sub>3</sub> is ___________.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Pbs, CuS, As2S3, CdS are soluble in 50% HNO3.

HgS, Sb2S3 are insoluble in 50% HNO3

So, answer is 4.

questions.questionText: The number of halogen/(s) forming halic (V) acid is ___________.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Except F and At, all other halide can form Halic (V) acid.

F cannot go in +5 oxidation state.

At is radioactive.

The number of halogen forming halic (V) acid

HClO3

HBrO3

HIO3

So answer is 3

questions.questionText: The molarity of the solution prepared by dissolving 6.3 g of oxalic acid (H<sub>2</sub>C<sub>2</sub>O<sub>4</sub>.2H<sub>2</sub>O) in 250 mL of water in mol L<sup>$$-$$1</sup> is x $$\times$$ 10<sup>$$-$$2</sup>. The value of x is _____________. (Nearest integer) [Atomic mass : H : 1.0, C : 12.0, O : 16.0]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Ge(Z = 32) in its ground state electronic configuration has x completely filled orbitals with m<sub>l</sub> = 0. The value of x is ___________.
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
Completely filled orbital with ml = 0 are

= 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1

= 7

So, answer is 7.

questions.questionText:
Let the equation of the plane, that passes through the point (1, 4, $$-$$3) and contains the line of intersection of the
planes 3x $$-$$ 2y + 4z $$-$$ 7 = 0
and x + 5y $$-$$ 2z + 9 = 0, be
$$\alpha$$x + $$\beta$$y + $$\gamma$$z + 3 = 0, then $$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ + $$\gamma$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
3x $$-$$ 2y + 4z $$-$$ 7 + $$\lambda$$(x + 5y $$-$$ 2z + 9) = 0

(3 + $$\lambda$$)x + (5$$\lambda$$ $$-$$ 2)y + (4 $$-$$ 2$$\lambda$$)z + 9$$\lambda$$ $$-$$ 7 = 0

passing through (1, 4, $$-$$3)

$$\Rightarrow$$ 3 + $$\lambda$$ + 20$$\lambda$$ $$-$$ 8 $$-$$ 12 + 6$$\lambda$$ + 9$$\lambda$$ $$-$$ 7 = 0

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\lambda$$ = $${2 \over 3}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ equation of plane is

$$-$$11x $$-$$ 4y $$-$$ 8z + 3 = 0

$$\Rightarrow$$ $$\alpha$$ + $$\beta$$ + $$\gamma$$ = $$-$$23

questions.questionText:
The number of real roots of the equation

$${e^{4x}} + 2{e^{3x}} - {e^x} - 6 = 0$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $${e^x} = t > 0$$

$$f(t) = {t^4} + 2{t^3} - t - 6 = 0$$

$$f'(t) = 4{t^3} + 6{t^2} - 1$$



$$f''(t) = 12{t^2} + 12t > 0$$



$$f(0) = - 6,f(1) = - 4,f(2) = 24$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ Number of real roots = 1

questions.questionText: Let f be a non-negative function in [0, 1] and twice differentiable in (0, 1). If $$\int_0^x {\sqrt {1 - {{(f'(t))}^2}} dt = \int_0^x {f(t)dt} } $$, $$0 \le x \le 1$$ and f(0) = 0, then $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {1 \over {{x^2}}}\int_0^x {f(t)dt} $$ :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\int_0^x {\sqrt {1 - {{(f'(t))}^2}} dt = \int_0^x {f(t)dt} } ,\,0 \le x \le 1$$

differentiating both the sides

$$\sqrt {1 - {{(f'(x))}^2}} = f(x)$$

$$ \Rightarrow 1 - {(f'(x))^2} = {f^2}(x)$$

$${{f'(x)} \over {\sqrt {1 - {f^2}(x)} }} = 1$$

$${\sin ^{ - 1}}f(x) = x + C$$

$$\because$$ $$f(0) = 0 \Rightarrow C = 0 \Rightarrow f(x) = \sin x$$

Now, $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{\int\limits_0^x {\sin t\,dt} } \over {{x^2}}}\left( {{0 \over 0}} \right) = {1 \over 2}$$

questions.questionText: If $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {{{2^{x + y}} - {2^x}} \over {{2^y}}}$$, y(0) = 1, then y(1) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The line $$12x\cos \theta  + 5y\sin \theta  = 60$$ is tangent to which of the following curves?
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$12x\cos \theta + 5y\sin \theta = 60$$

$${{x\cos \theta } \over 5} + {{y\sin \theta } \over {12}} = 1$$

is tangent to $${{{x^2}} \over {25}} + {{{y^2}} \over {144}} = 1$$

$$144{x^2} + 25{y^2} = 3600$$

questions.questionText: A vertical pole fixed to the horizontal ground is divided in the ratio 3 : 7 by a mark on it with lower part shorter than the upper part. If the two parts subtend equal angles at a point on the ground 18 m away from the base of the pole, then the height of the pole (in meters) is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let height of pole = 10$$l$$

$$\tan \alpha = {{3l} \over {18}} = {l \over 6}$$

$$\tan 2\alpha = {{10l} \over {18}}$$

$${{2\tan \alpha } \over {1 - {{\tan }^2}\alpha }} = {{10l} \over {18}}$$

use $$\tan \alpha = {l \over 6} \Rightarrow l = \sqrt {{{72} \over 5}} $$

height of pole = $$10l = 12\sqrt {10} $$

questions.questionText: The integral $$\int {{1 \over {\root 4 \of {{{(x - 1)}^3}{{(x + 2)}^5}} }}} \,dx$$ is equal to : (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The function

$$f(x) = \left| {{x^2} - 2x - 3} \right|\,.\,{e^{\left| {9{x^2} - 12x + 4} \right|}}$$ is not differentiable at exactly :
questions.subject: Mathematics
Mathematics
questions.solution:
If f(x) is continuous at x = 0, RHL = LHL = f(0)

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ + }} f(x) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ + }} {{{{\cos }^2}x - {{\sin }^2}x - 1} \over {\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} - 1}}.{{\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} + 1} \over {\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} + 1}}$$ (Rationalisation)

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ + }} - {{2{{\sin }^2}x} \over {{x^2}}}.\left( {\sqrt {{x^2} + 1} + 1} \right) = - 4$$

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ - }} f(x) = \mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ - }} {1 \over x}\ln \left( {{{1 + {x \over a}} \over {1 - {x \over b}}}} \right)$$

$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to {0^ - }} {{\ln \left( {1{x \over a}} \right)} \over {\left( {{x \over a}} \right).\,a}} + {{\ln \left( {1 - {x \over b}} \right)} \over {\left( { - {x \over b}} \right)\,.\,b}}$$$$ = {1 \over a} + {1 \over b}$$

So, $${1 \over a} + {1 \over b} = - 4 = k$$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over a} + {1 \over b} + {4 \over k} = - 4 - 1 = - 5$$

questions.questionText: $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{{{\sin }^2}\left( {\pi {{\cos }^4}x} \right)} \over {{x^4}}}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{{{\sin }^2}\left( {\pi {{\cos }^4}x} \right)} \over {{x^4}}}$$

= $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{1 - \cos \left( {2\pi {{\cos }^4}x} \right)} \over {2{x^4}}}$$

= $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0} {{1 - \cos \left( {2\pi - 2\pi {{\cos }^4}x} \right)} \over {{{\left[ {2\pi (1 - {{\cos }^4}x)} \right]}^2}}}4{\pi ^2}.{{{{\sin }^4}x} \over {2{x^4}}}{\left( {1 + {{\cos }^2}x} \right)^2}$$

$$ = {1 \over 2}.4{\pi ^2}.{1 \over 2}{(2)^2} = 4{\pi ^2}$$

questions.questionText: Let *, ▢ $$\in$${$$\wedge$$, $$\vee$$} be such that the Boolean expression (p * $$\sim$$ q) $$\Rightarrow$$ (p ▢ q) is a tautology. Then :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
(p $$\wedge$$ $$\sim$$ q) $$\to$$ (p $$\vee$$ q) is tautology

p q $$ \sim $$ q p $$ \wedge $$ $$ \sim $$ q p $$ \vee $$ q (p $$ \wedge $$ $$ \sim $$ q) $$ \to $$ (p $$ \vee $$ q)
T T F F T T
T F T T T T
F T F F T T
F F T F F T

questions.questionText: The length of the latus rectum of a parabola, whose vertex and focus are on the positive x-axis at a distance R and S (> R) respectively from the origin, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
V $$\to$$ Vertex

F $$\to$$ focus

VF = S $$-$$ R

So, latus rectum = 4(S $$-$$ R)

questions.questionText: cosec18$$^\circ$$ is a root of the equation :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$$\cos ec18^\circ = {1 \over {\sin 18^\circ }} = {4 \over {\sqrt 5 - 1}} = \sqrt 5 + 1$$

Let $$\cos ec18^\circ = x = \sqrt 5 + 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow x - 1 = \sqrt 5 $$

Squaring both sides, we get

$${x^2} - 2x + 1 = 5$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x - 4 = 0$$

questions.questionText:
The sum of 10 terms of the series

$${3 \over {{1^2} \times {2^2}}} + {5 \over {{2^2} \times {3^2}}} + {7 \over {{3^2} \times {4^2}}} + ....$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Three numbers are in an increasing geometric progression with common ratio r. If the middle number is doubled, then the new numbers are in an arithmetic progression with common difference d. If the fourth term of GP is 3 r<sup>2</sup>, then r<sup>2</sup> $$-$$ d is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let numbers be $${a \over r}$$, a, ar $$\to$$ G.P.

$${a \over r}$$, 2a, ar $$\to$$ A.P. $$\Rightarrow$$ 4a = $${a \over r}$$ + ar $$\Rightarrow$$ r + $${1 \over r}$$ = 4

r = 2 $$\pm$$ $$\sqrt 3 $$

4th form of G.P. = 3r2 $$\Rightarrow$$ ar2 = 3r2 $$\Rightarrow$$ a = 3

r = 2 + $$\sqrt 3 $$, a = 3, d = 2a $$-$$ $${a \over r}$$ = 3$$\sqrt 3 $$

r2 $$-$$ d = (2 + $$\sqrt 3 $$)2 $$-$$ 3$$\sqrt 3 $$

= 7 + 4$$\sqrt 3 $$ $$-$$ 3$$\sqrt 3 $$

= 7 + $$\sqrt 3 $$

questions.questionText: Which of the following is not correct for relation R on the set of real numbers ?
options: (x, y) $$\in$$ R $$ \Leftrightarrow $$ 0 < |x| $$-$$ |y| $$\le$$ 1 is neither transitive nor symmetric.
(x, y) $$\in$$ R $$ \Leftrightarrow $$ 0 < |x $$-$$ y| $$\le$$ 1 is symmetric and transitive.
(x, y) $$\in$$ R $$ \Leftrightarrow $$ |x| $$-$$ |y| $$\le$$ 1 is reflexive but not symmetric.
(x, y) $$\in$$ R $$ \Leftrightarrow $$ |x $$-$$ y| $$\le$$ 1 is reflexive nd symmetric.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Note that (a, b) and (b, c) satisfy 0 < |x $$-$$ y| $$\le$$ 1 but (a, c) does not satisfy it so 0 $$\le$$ |x $$-$$ y| $$\le$$ 1 is symmetric but not transitive.

For example,

x = 0.2, y = 0.9, z = 1.5

0 ≤ |x – y| = 0.7 ≤ 1

0 ≤ |y – z| = 0.6 ≤ 1

But |x – z| = 1.3 > 1

So, (b) is correct.

questions.questionText:
If p and q are the lengths of the perpendiculars from the origin on the lines,

x cosec $$\alpha$$ $$-$$ y sec $$\alpha$$ = k cot 2$$\alpha$$ and

x sin$$\alpha$$ + y cos$$\alpha$$ = k sin2$$\alpha$$

respectively, then k2 is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
First line is $${x \over {\sin \alpha }} - {y \over {\cos \alpha }} = {{k\cos 2\alpha } \over {\sin 2\alpha }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x\cos \alpha - y\sin \alpha = {k \over 2}\cos 2\alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow p = \left| {{k \over 2}\cos \alpha } \right| \Rightarrow 2p = \left| {k\cos 2\alpha } \right|$$ .... (i)

second line is $$x\sin \alpha + y\cos \alpha = k\sin 2\alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow q = \left| {k\sin 2\alpha } \right|$$ .... (ii)

Hence, $$4{p^2} + {q^2} = {k^2}$$ (From (i) & (ii))

questions.questionText:
Let $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow b $$ be two vectors
such that $$\left| {2\overrightarrow a + 3\overrightarrow b } \right| = \left| {3\overrightarrow a + \overrightarrow b } \right|$$ and the angle between $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow b $$ is 60$$^\circ$$. If $${1 \over 8}\overrightarrow a $$ is a unit vector, then $$\left| {\overrightarrow b } \right|$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The square of the distance of the point of intersection

of the line $${{x - 1} \over 2} = {{y - 2} \over 3} = {{z + 1} \over 6}$$ and the plane $$2x - y + z = 6$$ from the point ($$-$$1, $$-$$1, 2) is __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
$${{x - 1} \over 2} = {{y - 2} \over 3} = {{z + 1} \over 6} = \lambda $$

$$x = 2\lambda + 1,y = 3\lambda + 2,z = 6\lambda - 1$$

for point of intersection of line & plane

$$2(2\lambda + 1) - (3\lambda + 2) + (6\lambda - 1) = 6$$

$$7\lambda = 7 \Rightarrow \lambda = 1$$

point : (3, 5, 5)

(distance)2 = $${(3 + 1)^2} + {(5 + 1)^2} + {(5 - 2)^2}$$

$$ = 16 + 36 + 9 = 61$$

questions.questionText:
If 'R' is the least value of 'a' such that the function f(x) = x2 + ax + 1 is increasing on [1, 2] and 'S' is the greatest value of 'a' such that the function f(x) = x2 + ax + 1 is decreasing on [1, 2], then
the value of |R $$-$$ S| is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
f(x) = x2 + ax + 1

f'(x) = 2x + a

when f(x) is increasing on [1, 2]

2x + a $$\ge$$ 0 $$\forall$$ x$$\in$$[1, 2]

a $$\ge$$ $$-$$2x $$\forall$$ x$$\in$$[1, 2]

R = $$-$$4

when f(x) is decreasing on [1, 2]

2x + a $$\le$$ 0 $$\forall$$ x$$\in$$[1, 2]

a $$\le$$ $$-$$2 $$\forall$$ x$$\in$$[1, 2]

S = $$-$$2

|R $$-$$ S| = | $$-$$4 + 2 | = 2

questions.questionText: If $$\left( {{{{3^6}} \over {{4^4}}}} \right)k$$ is the term, independent of x, in the binomial expansion of $${\left( {{x \over 4} - {{12} \over {{x^2}}}} \right)^{12}}$$, then k is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the variable line 3x + 4y = $$\alpha$$ lies between the two
circles (x $$-$$ 1)2 + (y $$-$$ 1)2 = 1
and (x $$-$$ 9)2 + (y $$-$$ 1)2 = 4, without intercepting a chord on either circle, then the sum of all the integral values of $$\alpha$$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Both centers should lie on either side of the line as well as line can be tangent to circle.

(3 + 4 $$-$$ $$\alpha$$) . (27 + 4 $$-$$ $$\alpha$$) < 0

(7 $$-$$ $$\alpha$$) . (31 $$-$$ $$\alpha$$) < 0 $$\Rightarrow$$ $$\alpha$$ $$\in$$ (7, 31) ....... (1)

d1 = distance of (1, 1) from line

d2 = distance of (9, 1) from line

$${d_1} \ge {r_1} \Rightarrow {{|7 - \alpha |} \over 5} \ge 1 \Rightarrow \alpha \in ( - \infty ,2] \cup [12,\infty )$$ .... (2)

$${d_2} \ge {r_2} \Rightarrow {{|31 - \alpha |} \over 5} \ge 2 \Rightarrow \alpha \in ( - \infty ,21] \cup [41,\infty )$$ ....(3)

(1) $$\cap$$ (2) $$\cap$$ (3) $$\Rightarrow$$ $$\alpha$$ $$\in$$ [12, 21]

Sum of integers = 165

questions.questionText: A point z moves in the complex plane such that $$\arg \left( {{{z - 2} \over {z + 2}}} \right) = {\pi  \over 4}$$, then the minimum value of $${\left| {z - 9\sqrt 2  - 2i} \right|^2}$$ is equal to _______________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Let $$z = x + iy$$

$$\arg \left( {{{x - 2 + iy} \over {x + 2 + iy}}} \right) = {\pi \over 4}$$

$$\arg (x - 2 + iy) - \arg (x + 2 + iy) = {\pi \over 4}$$

$${\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{y \over {x - 2}}} \right) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}\left( {{y \over {x + 2}}} \right) = {\pi \over 4}$$

$${{{y \over {x - 2}} - {y \over {x + 2}}} \over {1 + \left( {{y \over {x - 2}}} \right).\left( {{y \over {x + 2}}} \right)}} = \tan {\pi \over 4} = 1$$

$${{xy + 2y - xy + 2y} \over {{x^2} - 4 + {y^2}}} = 1$$

$$4y = {x^2} - 4 + {y^2}$$

$${x^2} + {y^2} - 4y - 4 = 0$$

locus is a circle with center (0, 2) & radius = $$2\sqrt 2 $$



min. value = $${(AP)^2} = {(OP - OA)^2}$$

$$ = {\left( {9\sqrt 2 - 2\sqrt 2 } \right)^2}$$

$$ = {\left( {7\sqrt 2 } \right)^2} = 98$$

questions.questionText:
Let [t] denote the greatest integer $$\le$$ t. Then the value of

$$8.\int\limits_{ - {1 \over 2}}^1 {([2x] + |x|)dx} $$ is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of six letter words (with or without meaning), formed using all the letters of the word 'VOWELS', so that all the consonants never come together, is ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
Total possible words = 6! = 720

When 4 consonants are together (V, W, L, S)
such cases = 3! ⋅ 4! = 144

All consonants should not be together

= Total $$-$$ All consonants together,

= 6! $$-$$ 3! 4! = 576

questions.questionText: An electric instrument consists of two units. Each unit must function independently for the instrument to operate. The probability that the first unit functions is 0.9 and that of the second unit is 0.8. The instrument is switched on and it fails to operate. If the probability that only the first unit failed and second unit is functioning is p, then 98 p is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.solution:
I1 = first unit is functioning

I2 = second unit is functioning

P(I1) = 0.9, P(I2) = 0.8

P($$\overline {{I_1}} $$) = 0.1, P($$\overline {{I_2}} $$) = 0.2

$$P = {{0.8 \times 0.1} \over {0.1 \times 0.2 + 0.9 \times 0.2 + 0.1 \times 0.8}} = {8 \over {28}}$$

$$98P = {8 \over {28}} \times 98 = 28$$
